channel.title: Drupal Core
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Core.

item.title: #3592887: AttributeRouteDiscovery does not cleanly handle invalid classes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +76/−5 · php +74/−5 · dist +2
  • Diff: b0d5f3a
  • Discussion: #3592887 · 4 contributors · 25 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Sites running modules that include "compatibility shim" controllers, such as TFA 1.x on Drupal 11.4, were seeing a PHP fatal error during cache clear or drush cr. The attribute-based route discovery introduced in #3311365 scanned every PHP class file in a module and tried to autoload it. If a class had an incompatible method signature relative to its parent, PHP raised a fatal error that was not caught, bringing down the routing system. This fix makes the discovery process skip such classes gracefully instead of crashing.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Two changes land in AttributeRouteDiscovery. First, a fast pre-filter: before autoloading a class, the file's raw contents are read and checked with str_contains() for the strings #[Route or Routing\Attribute\Route. Files that match neither string are skipped entirely, avoiding the autoloader for the vast majority of classes. The approach has acknowledged edge cases (a comment containing #[Route is a false positive) but these are an accepted tradeoff since route discovery results are cached. Second, the class_exists() call inside createRouteCollection() is wrapped in a try/catch (\Error) block so that classes which do pass the string filter but still fail to autoload (e.g., a class extending a parent from a missing module) are silently skipped instead of propagating a fatal error. The catch targets \Error specifically; the commit comment notes that missing traits only became catchable as \Error in PHP 8.5, so the test fixture for this case uses a missing parent class instead, which is catchable on all supported PHP versions. Two intentionally invalid test fixtures are added to router_test_directory: TestInvalidController (an abstract method on a non-abstract class) and TestMissingDependency (a #[Route]-annotated class that extends a class from a non-existent module). Both are excluded from PHPStan. The broader challenge of centralizing fatal-error handling across all attribute discovery subsystems is tracked separately in #3586328.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 30, 2026
  • First commit: June 2, 2026 (2 days later)
  • Last commit: June 3, 2026 (1 day and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • cmlara (DM13 Security LLC)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • godotislate
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

longwave proposed the str_contains() pre-filter approach after a Slack discussion with godotislate and catch (noted by godotislate in comment #16602879), then implemented it in MR !15945. cmlara, the issue reporter, tested the MR against TFA's codebase and confirmed the fix resolved the crash, while also flagging the false-positive risk of the string-matching approach and noting that \Throwable catches the load failure on PHP 8.4.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3589626: clickLink in kernel tests erroneously prefixes some paths with /
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +7/−4 · php +7/−4
  • Diff: b921ffe
  • Discussion: #3589626 · 5 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

A bug in HttpKernelUiHelperTrait::drupalGet() caused clickLink() in kernel tests to navigate to the wrong URL when the clicked link's href was a bare query string (for example ?page=1). The old code prepended / to any path not starting with /, turning ?page=1 into /?page=1 and sending the test to the front page instead of the intended page. The fix teaches drupalGet() to leave query-string paths alone so they resolve relative to the current page URL as expected.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

In HttpKernelUiHelperTrait::drupalGet(), the guard that adds a leading / to bare relative paths now also checks whether the path starts with ?. The original condition !str_starts_with($path, '/') was expanded to !str_starts_with($path, '?') && !str_starts_with($path, '/'), so paths like ?page=1 pass through unchanged and are resolved relative to the current page URL by the underlying Symfony browser client. The @param docblock for $path was updated to document this distinction. This trait was introduced in #3582769 and getUrl() was added in #3583594.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 11, 2026
  • Committed: June 3, 2026 (22 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mstrelan
  • joachim (Chicken)
  • dww (3281d Consulting)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #1499532: Respect explicitly set node path alias langcode on save
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +49/−5 · php +49/−5
  • Diff: f30d64c
  • Discussion: #1499532 · 10 contributors · 39 comments
  • Followers: 16

Summary

On multilingual sites, setting a path alias to a specific language (or to language-neutral, LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED) programmatically was silently ignored when saving a node. The alias was always stored with the node translation's language instead. A langcode-only update (same alias text, different language) was also dropped entirely. Both cases are now fixed: the explicitly provided alias langcode is respected on both create and update.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Two bugs lived in PathItem::postSave() inside core/modules/path/src/Plugin/Field/FieldType/PathItem.php.

First, the langcode selection used the condition ($langcode && $pid) ? $langcode : $this->getLangcode(). Because $pid is absent when creating a new alias, any explicit langcode supplied at node-creation time was always discarded in favour of the entity's own langcode. The fix simplifies this to $langcode ?: $this->getLangcode(), so any non-empty explicit langcode wins for both new and existing aliases.

Second, the update branch only called $path_alias->save() when the alias text changed ($alias != $path_alias->getAlias()). A change to the langcode alone was never persisted. The fix introduces a $path_alias_changed flag that is set when either the alias text or the langcode ($alias_langcode !== $path_alias->language()->getId()) differs from the stored value, and save is triggered when either is true.

A third, related fix is in PathDefaultWidget::formElement(). The hidden langcode form field previously always forwarded $items[$delta]->langcode, which for unsaved items resolved to the entity's langcode and would have overridden any programmatic langcode set before the form was rendered. It now passes NULL when no $pid exists yet (new alias), allowing the save logic to apply the explicitly set value instead.

New kernel test cases in PathItemTest::testPathItem() cover: setting langcode to LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED on a saved node's path field and re-saving (verifies the language-neutral alias is stored and the language-specific alias is gone), and creating a new node with LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED in the path data (verifies the alias is created language-neutral from the start).

Upgrade

No API changes. If your code was previously working around this bug by manually editing the path alias after node save, that workaround can be removed. For example, code like:

// Before: workaround needed because langcode was ignored on save
$node->path->langcode = LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED;
$node->save();
// Then manually fix the stored alias…
$alias = $alias_storage->load($pid);
$alias->set('langcode', LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED);
$alias->save();

can now be simplified to:

// After: langcode is respected on save
$node->path->langcode = LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED;
$node->save();

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 25, 2012
  • Committed: June 2, 2026 (14 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • q0rban
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • joelpittet (The University of British Columbia)

Collaboration

joelpittet picked up this 13-year-old issue after smustgrave flagged it as a daily BSI target, noting that the original patch referenced $node->path['language'], which had long since become $node->path->langcode in modern Drupal. joelpittet opened MR !15411, rewrote the issue summary to the standard template, and disclosed in the thread that they used AI as an assistive tool while remaining the human reviewer: they questioned the extra $path_alias_changed flag and ran the tests both with and without the fix to confirm the red/green results before submitting. smustgrave independently verified the fix by running a drush php:eval snippet against a fresh install with two languages and confirmed the alias langcode changed from en to und with the patch applied.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3549601: Allow --directory and @group to work together in run-tests.sh
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +103/−19 · php +64/−14 · sh +39/−5
  • Diff: db775a3
  • Discussion: #3549601 · 6 contributors · 65 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

When running core/scripts/run-tests.sh with both the --directory option and a positional @group argument, the group filter was previously ignored and all tests in the directory were run. This made it impossible for contrib module maintainers to do the two things at once: limit test discovery to their module's directory (which avoids fatal errors from unrelated third-party modules) and still run only a specific test group. The fix makes the two options work together: --directory narrows the file scan and the group argument then filters the discovered tests down to the requested subset. The script now also prints a "Test discovery" header summarizing which directory, module, test suite(s), and group(s) are active for the run.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause was that in Drupal 11.2+ the run-tests.sh script adopted PHPUnit 11's test discovery API (PhpUnitTestDiscovery). When --directory was in use, getTestClasses() called the getTestListLimitedToDirectory() code path, but the positional group argument from Config::getTests() was never forwarded to the discovery layer; it was only used in the separate "groups-only" code path. The fix adds a $testGroups parameter to the public PhpUnitTestDiscovery::getTestClasses() and findAllClassFiles() methods, and to the private helpers getTestList() and getTestListLimitedToDirectory(). Both helpers now apply an array_intersect check against $item['groups'] and skip any test class whose groups do not overlap with the requested list. The call sites in run-tests.sh (simpletest_script_get_test_list() and the --list-files-json path) are updated to pass Config::getTests() as $testGroups. A new guard in Config::createFromCommandLine() throws a RuntimeException when --directory is combined with --class or --file, since those options are all file-based and mutually exclusive. This fix was built on top of the Symfony Console-based argument parsing introduced in #3536964.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 30, 2025
  • Committed: June 2, 2026 (8 months and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • jonathan1055
  • mondrake
  • godotislate
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

The initial implementation by jonathan1055 kept the group-filtering logic inside run-tests.sh. mondrake reviewed and proposed moving the filter into PhpUnitTestDiscovery itself, arguing it made the logic more testable and fit the ongoing effort to strip complexity out of run-tests.sh. That restructuring landed as a separate commit on the same MR. After a subsequent rebase dropped part of the implementation, jonathan1055 noticed by testing against a real contrib pipeline (the webform module) that group filtering had silently stopped working again. mondrake identified the dropped logic, fixed it, and added kernel tests that verify that --directory plus a group argument returns strictly fewer results than --directory alone. godotislate independently pulled the branch and ran the full combination of options locally, posting detailed output to confirm correctness before the final commit.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3587564: Move search functionality from node to Search module
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 49 files, +1124/−992 · php +1103/−978 · yml +21/−14
  • Diff: bcb6694
  • Discussion: #3587564 · 3 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

A new search_node core submodule now owns all of the Search module integration that was previously bundled inside the Node module. Sites that use the built-in content search (the node_search search page) will need this new module enabled. An update hook handles the transition automatically on existing sites. This work is part of the broader effort to deprecate the Search module by extracting its integrations into dedicated submodules.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: \Drupal\node\Plugin\Search\NodeSearch deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:12.0.0; use \Drupal\search_node\Plugin\Search\SearchNode instead.
  • New API: New search_node core submodule introduced, owning the node_search plugin, its hooks, config, and Views data integration.

Technical details

The canonical node_search plugin class has moved from core/modules/node/src/Plugin/Search/NodeSearch.php (\Drupal\node\Plugin\Search\NodeSearch) to core/modules/search/modules/search_node/src/Plugin/Search/SearchNode.php (\Drupal\search_node\Plugin\Search\SearchNode). The old class is retained as a deprecated shell that simply extends the new one and fires E_USER_DEPRECATED in its constructor; the #[Search(id: 'node_search')] attribute now lives on SearchNode.

Several hooks previously implemented inside the Node module have been relocated. NodeSearchHooks (cron rankings aggregation) was re-namespaced into Drupal\search_node\Hook. A new SearchNodeHooks class implements hook_entity_predelete (clearing the search index when a node is deleted), hook_node_update (clearing removed translations from the index), hook_entity_view_display_alter (hiding field labels in the search_index view mode), and a hook_form_node_preview_form_select_alter to unset the search_index view mode option. The Views data for node_search_index, node_search_total, and node_search_dataset was extracted from NodeViewsData::getViewsData() into a new SearchNodeViewsHooks::viewsDataAlter() hook.

On the configuration side, the search.page.node_search.yml optional config file now declares search_node (not node) as its required module; the schema entry for search.plugin.node_search moved from node.schema.yml to a new search_node.schema.yml; and the core.entity_view_mode.node.search_index and core.entity_view_mode.node.search_result install configs moved from the Node module to search_node. The Node entity class itself was cleaned up: the preDelete() static method and the translation-removal call in postSave() were deleted, since those responsibilities now live in SearchNodeHooks.

Upgrade

Code that subclasses or type-hints \Drupal\node\Plugin\Search\NodeSearch must be updated:

// Before (deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:12.0.0):
use Drupal\node\Plugin\Search\NodeSearch;

// After:
use Drupal\search_node\Plugin\Search\SearchNode;

The search_node module must be enabled wherever the node_search search page is in use. On existing sites an update hook handles this automatically, but custom install profiles or test environments that enable modules explicitly should add search_node to their module list.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 29, 2026
  • First commit: May 26, 2026 (27 days later)
  • Last commit: June 2, 2026 (7 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #2875033: Optimize joins and table selection in SQL entity query implementation
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 9 files, +205/−34 · php +205/−34
  • Diff: 702159f
  • Discussion: #2875033 · 36 contributors · 119 comments
  • Followers: 87

Summary

The SQL entity query layer now generates significantly fewer joins and avoids unnecessary GROUP BY clauses in many common scenarios. Previously, querying a base-table property like uuid would join the table to itself; querying two fields from the data table would add two independent joins to the same table; following an entity reference with multiple conditions would repeat the reference join for each condition; and any entity type with a data table would unconditionally mark the query as non-simple, adding a GROUP BY even when only one row per entity was possible. All of these redundancies are fixed. Sites with large datasets or many translations should see measurable query-time improvements for entity queries, including those driven by JSON:API and Views.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: ConditionInterface requires two new methods setQuery() and getQuery(); Query::getTables() no longer accepts a $sql_query argument (deprecated drupal:11.4.0, removed drupal:13.0.0).
  • Deprecation: Query::getTables($sql_query) with an argument and passing a Condition from a different query into condition() are both deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0.
  • New API: LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_DEFAULT is now a valid langcode argument to entity-query condition() calls; it joins data tables on default_langcode = 1 rather than a literal language match.

Technical details

The root cause was that Query::getTables() created a fresh Tables instance on every call, so each nested Condition (inside AND/OR groups) got its own join-tracking state and could not deduplicate against joins already added by sibling conditions. The fix introduces doGetTables(), which stores and reuses a single Tables instance per query. All Condition objects that belong to the same query now share one Tables instance, so the entityTables and fieldTables arrays correctly prevent the same join from being added twice.

Five specific join-redundancy bugs are fixed by this shared state:

  1. Base-table self-join: prepare() stores the base table name in query metadata under entity_query_base_table. The Tables constructor pre-populates entityTables with that entry, so a condition on a base-table column (e.g., uuid) resolves directly to base_table instead of joining the table to itself.

  2. Data-table joins: ensureEntityTable() now keys entries by $index_prefix . $base_table . '.' . $table . ($langcode ? '.' . $langcode : ''), properly deduplicating joins regardless of which condition requests them. The old key was incorrect when the base table differed from 'base_table'.

  3. Entity-reference joins: addNextBaseTable() now caches its result in a nextBaseTables array keyed by $table.$sql_column, so following the same reference path in two conditions produces one join instead of two.

  4. simple_query / GROUP BY elimination: prepare() now always starts with simple_query = TRUE. Inside Tables::addField(), the flag is only set to FALSE when the data-table join has no langcode restriction (a langcode-restricted join is 1-to-1, so no fan-out occurs). Queries with a specific language condition or with a single-cardinality field no longer add GROUP BY.

  5. Multi-value AND conditions: A new tableIndexPrefix property on Condition ensures that each top-level andConditionGroup() wrapping a multi-value field gets its own index namespace. This keeps the well-documented behaviour (placing ->andConditionGroup()->condition('field', 'X') and ->andConditionGroup()->condition('field', 'Y') as siblings to find entities that have both values) while still deduplicating joins within a single group.

Additionally, LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_DEFAULT is now recognised in addJoin(). When it is passed for a data table or revision data table, the join condition uses default_langcode = 1 rather than a literal language-code match, allowing callers to query against the entity's default language without knowing its code.

Two deprecations ship with this change: passing $sql_query to Query::getTables() is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0 (use doGetTables() or, for custom subclasses, override doGetTables()); passing a Condition created by a different query instance to another query's condition() is also deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0.

ConditionInterface gains two new required methods, setQuery(QueryInterface $query) and getQuery(), which the base ConditionBase class implements by propagating the query reference recursively through nested conditions.

Upgrade

Implementations of \Drupal\Core\Entity\Query\ConditionInterface must add two new methods:

public function setQuery(QueryInterface $query): static {
    $this->query = $query;
    foreach ($this->conditions as $condition) {
        if ($condition['field'] instanceof ConditionInterface) {
            $condition['field']->setQuery($query);
        }
    }
    return $this;
}

public function getQuery(): QueryInterface {
    return $this->query;
}

If your code calls getTables() with a $sql_query argument, remove it:

// Before (deprecated):
$tables = $this->query->getTables($this->sqlQuery);

// After:
$tables = $this->query->doGetTables();

If your code reuses a condition group from one query inside a different query, stop doing so:

// Deprecated pattern – $query1 and $query2 are separate instances:
$group = $query1->andConditionGroup()->condition('field', 'value');
$query2->condition($group);

// Correct – always create condition groups from the query you are building:
$group = $query2->andConditionGroup()->condition('field', 'value');
$query2->condition($group);

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 2, 2017
  • Committed: June 2, 2026 (9 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • driskell (Other Media)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • amateescu
  • kaythay (Aten Design Group)
  • joachim
  • rlmumford
  • heddn (MTech, LLC)
  • ghost of drupal past
  • alexbrut (Smile)
  • etroid
  • andreyks (Adyax)
  • cilefen
  • damondt
  • davidwhthomas
  • itsekhmistro (Adyax)
  • mstef
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

driskell drove the final implementation that landed. After years of stalled patches, they rewrote the approach from first principles on 11.x: introduced the shared Tables singleton, tableIndexPrefix for multi-value AND semantics, doGetTables(), LANGCODE_DEFAULT join support, and the setQuery()/getQuery() API. In comment #71 they surfaced the obscure andConditionGroup cross-delta behaviour as a design question and clearly documented why preserving it required the prefix approach, then in comment #74 they committed to a follow-up to clean it up rather than let it block the fix. joachim provided the framing in comment #75 that confirmed this was a refactoring issue and that the andConditionGroup question should stay separate.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3593008: AccessPolicyProcessor::processAccessPolicies() creates unnecessary Fibers when checking permissions for the current user
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +7/−3 · php +7/−3
  • Diff: 29aa27a
  • Discussion: #3593008 · 3 contributors · 12 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

A redundant Fiber was being created on every permission check that ran inside an existing Fiber, even when checking the current user's own permissions. On a typical page render with roughly 1,000 permission checks per request, the vast majority of those checks are for the current user. This fix eliminates those unnecessary Fibers, reducing Fiber::start calls by approximately 90% per page and saving roughly 15–25 ms per cold render.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The Fiber wrapping in AccessPolicyProcessor::processAccessPolicies() was introduced by #3576074 to isolate accountSwitcher->switchTo() calls and prevent them from leaking the switched account identity to code outside the Fiber. However, an account switch only occurs in doProcessAccessPolicies() when the requested account differs from the current user. The original guard checked only !\Fiber::getCurrent(), so any call from inside a Fiber, regardless of which account was being checked, would spin up a new child Fiber.

The fix adds a second short-circuit condition: $account->id() === $this->currentUser->id(). When either condition is true (not in a Fiber, or checking the current user), doProcessAccessPolicies() is called directly without creating a \Fiber wrapper. A child Fiber is now only created when code is already running inside a Fiber and the account being checked is a different user, the only situation where the account-switch isolation actually matters.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 1, 2026
  • Committed: June 1, 2026

Key contributors

  • herved (European Commission and European Union Institutions, Agencies and Bodies)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586951: Remove Settings Tray integration in Default Admin, keep in Stable 9
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +0/−136 · yml +0/−12 · css +0/−124
  • Diff: b090035
  • Discussion: #3586951 · 6 contributors · 32 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

The default_admin theme shipped two CSS libraries (settings_tray and settings_tray_edit) intended to style the Settings Tray edit mode, but those styles never had any effect: default_admin strips contextual links from all non-layout-builder blocks, which is the only mechanism that opens the Settings Tray. The dead CSS files and library definitions have now been removed. The corresponding integration in Stable 9 is intentionally left alone, since Stable 9 is itself being removed from Drupal core in Drupal 12.

Impact

Affects front-end developers.

  • Theme change: Libraries default_admin/settings_tray and default_admin/settings_tray_edit removed from the default_admin theme; any external code depending on them must remove those dependencies.

Technical details

The default_admin theme declared two library entries (settings_tray and settings_tray_edit) in default_admin.libraries.yml, each wrapping a pair of CSS files under migration/css/components/. These libraries were attached to the navigation and toolbar library groups. The theme's preprocessBlock hook (which removes contextual links from all blocks except layout builder blocks, inherited originally from the Seven theme via #2487025) makes it impossible to launch the Settings Tray on any admin-theme block, so the styling was unreachable.

The commit removes the four CSS files (settings_tray.css, settings_tray_edit.css, and their minified counterparts), drops the two library definitions, and removes the default_admin/settings_tray and default_admin/settings_tray_edit entries from the navigation and toolbar dependency lists. The Stable 9 settings_tray/drupal.settings_tray overrides in stable9.info.yml are untouched because Stable 9 is scheduled for removal from core itself.

Upgrade

Any custom module or theme that declares a dependency on the removed libraries must be updated:

# Before (now broken)
dependencies:
  - default_admin/settings_tray
  - default_admin/settings_tray_edit

These libraries no longer exist and should be removed from your dependency declarations. Because Settings Tray does not function in admin themes (contextual links are suppressed there), no replacement dependency is available within default_admin.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 26, 2026
  • Committed: June 1, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • mherchel (Dripyard)
  • dcam
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

During review, dcam discovered that the original procedural hook implementation for loading the Settings Tray libraries was silently not firing. Investigating why led to the broader finding, confirmed by longwave, that Settings Tray is entirely non-functional in admin themes because default_admin and Claro both implement hook_preprocess_block to remove contextual links from all blocks. Longwave traced this back to #2487025, which removed contextual links from the Seven theme, with Claro and Default Admin later inheriting that behavior. This discovery shifted the resolution from moving the CSS into the settings_tray module to simply removing it entirely from default_admin.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591509: Remove Migrate Drupal settings from settings.php
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +0/−110 · php +0/−110
  • Diff: 6173c05
  • Discussion: #3591509 · 4 contributors · 10 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Five $settings keys related to Migrate Drupal have been removed from default.settings.php. These keys were used exclusively by the Migrate Drupal module, which was removed from core in #3572280. If your settings.php still defines any of them, they will be silently ignored; you can safely remove them.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: Five Migrate Drupal $settings keys removed from default.settings.php; any definitions still present in settings.php are now silently ignored.

Technical details

The commit removes two blocks from both core/assets/scaffold/files/default.settings.php and sites/default/default.settings.php. The first block was an active assignment, $settings['migrate_node_migrate_type_classic'] = FALSE, which forced the classic node migration path in the Migrate Drupal upgrade wizard. The second block was a set of commented-out template entries for $settings['migrate_source_connection'], $settings['migrate_source_version'], $settings['migrate_file_public_path'], and $settings['migrate_file_private_path'], which supplied default values to the /upgrade/credentials credential form. With migrate_drupal removed from core (#3572280), no code in core reads any of these keys, making the settings stubs dead documentation.

Upgrade

If your settings.php still defines any of the following keys from a past D6/D7 migration, they are now unused and can be removed:

$settings['migrate_node_migrate_type_classic'] = FALSE;
$settings['migrate_source_connection'] = '';
$settings['migrate_source_version'] = '';
$settings['migrate_file_public_path'] = '';
$settings['migrate_file_private_path'] = '';

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 22, 2026
  • Committed: June 1, 2026 (9 days later)

Key contributors

  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • avinash.jha (OpenSense Labs)
  • dcam
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582690: Remove or replace usages of Shortcut from themes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 30 files, +143/−377 · php +124/−28 · yml +16/−37 · css +2/−299 · twig +1/−1 · svg +0/−12
  • Diff: 0d59982
  • Discussion: #3582690 · 7 contributors · 44 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

As groundwork for moving the Shortcut module to contributed modules, the Shortcut-specific CSS and preprocessing logic that was previously bundled with the Claro, Olivero, and default_admin themes has been relocated into the Shortcut module itself. Sites using Shortcut with the Claro or Olivero theme will see no visible change, but the CSS is now delivered by the module rather than by the theme. The default_admin theme has had all Shortcut styling removed outright, since Shortcut display there is handled by the Navigation module. A post-update function cleans up a stale third_party_settings.shortcut config entry from olivero.settings on existing sites.

Impact

Affects front-end developers and site owners.

  • Database update: olivero_post_update_remove_shortcut_settings_if_not_installed clears third_party_settings.shortcut from olivero.settings on upgrade.
  • Config change: olivero.schema.yml no longer declares a schema for third_party_settings.shortcut.module_link; the key is absent from olivero.settings when Shortcut is not installed.
  • Theme change: Shortcut CSS for Claro and Olivero is now owned and delivered by the Shortcut module via claro.shortcut.backport and olivero.shortcut.backport libraries; claro/drupal.shortcut no longer exists. The default_admin theme has had all Shortcut-specific CSS, icons, and template classes removed.

Technical details

Claro's drupal.shortcut library (defined in claro.libraries.yml with css/components/shortcut.css) and its shortcut/drupal.shortcut entry in libraries-extend are removed. The CSS moves to css/backport-themes/claro/shortcut.css inside the Shortcut module, served via the new claro.shortcut.backport library. Similarly, Olivero's css/components/page-title.css (which contained shortcut-wrapper positioning styles) is removed from Olivero's global-styling library; it moves to css/backport-themes/olivero/page-title.css in the Shortcut module, served via olivero.shortcut.backport. Both libraries are attached dynamically in ShortcutThemeHooks::preprocessPageTitle() when the active theme is claro or olivero, replacing the old libraries-extend mechanism.

Olivero's OliveroHooks::preprocessPageTitle() hook, which wrapped the page title and shortcut link in a positioning div, is deleted. The equivalent logic moves into the new ShortcutThemeHooks::preprocessPageTitleOlivero() method, called conditionally from ShortcutThemeHooks::preprocessPageTitle(). ShortcutThemeHooks gains a ThemeManagerInterface constructor injection to support this. Shortcut's shortcut_install() and shortcut_uninstall() functions now manage third_party_settings.shortcut.module_link on Olivero in addition to Claro. Olivero previously hardcoded module_link: true in its config/install/olivero.settings.yml and declared the key in its schema; both are removed. olivero_post_update_remove_shortcut_settings_if_not_installed() clears the stale third_party_settings.shortcut key from olivero.settings on upgrade.

The default_admin theme loses its drupal.shortcut library definition, its shortcut/drupal.shortcut library extension, shortcut icon SVG files, --admin-icon-shortcut and --admin-icon-shortcut-filled CSS custom properties, all .shortcut-action CSS rules, .toolbar-icon-shortcut / .toolbar-button--icon--shortcuts icon selectors, #toolbar-item-shortcuts visibility rules, and the menu--shortcuts class from menu-region--top.html.twig. Stable9 and Claro are intentionally left in place pending their respective deprecations.

Upgrade

If you maintain a sub-theme of Claro that previously overrode claro/drupal.shortcut via libraries-extend, that library no longer exists. Override shortcut/claro.shortcut.backport instead, or provide your own shortcut CSS.

If you maintain a sub-theme of Olivero that overrode the page-title shortcut wrapper styles, those styles are no longer in Olivero's global-styling library. They are now attached by the Shortcut module via shortcut/olivero.shortcut.backport.

If any code reads olivero.settings:third_party_settings.shortcut.module_link directly, the key is removed from the schema and will be cleared by the post-update function olivero_post_update_remove_shortcut_settings_if_not_installed. The setting is now managed entirely by the Shortcut module's install/uninstall hooks.

If you use the default_admin theme with Shortcut enabled, the theme-level shortcut CSS (icon, action link styling) is gone. The Navigation module continues to handle shortcut display; theme-level star-icon support is not present in default_admin.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 1, 2026
  • Committed: May 30, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • dcam
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

The approach was debated in the thread. smustgrave noted uncertainty about the right direction. longwave analyzed the codebase and identified the core problem: Olivero was setting third_party_settings.shortcut.module_link in its own install config rather than having Shortcut set it (as Claro already did), and theme CSS needed to move into the module. longwave outlined the solution in comment #27: Shortcut should manage the setting itself via install/uninstall hooks for all supported themes, CSS should move into Shortcut-owned libraries, and default_admin could drop Shortcut support entirely since Navigation handles display there. dcam identified a missing update function for existing Olivero config, prompting the addition of olivero_post_update_remove_shortcut_settings_if_not_installed. nicxvan caught that Olivero also needed an uninstall-time cleanup when Shortcut is removed.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3589208: Always use the file contents to determine asset aggregate filename hashes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +8/−6 · php +8/−6
  • Diff: 4def42a
  • Discussion: #3589208 · 5 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Drupal's asset aggregation system now always derives aggregate filename hashes from the actual file contents rather than from library version strings. Previously, core libraries all declared VERSION (the global Drupal version constant), so every patch release invalidated every aggregate file even when the underlying CSS or JS files had not changed. Conversely, a contrib or custom module that changed a file without bumping its library version would silently serve stale aggregates. After this fix, aggregate filenames change when and only when file contents change, making the cache more stable under core upgrades and more reliable under file edits.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The logic lived in CssCollectionOptimizerLazy->generateHash(). The old code only replaced the version with a content hash when $asset['version'] === -1 (meaning no version was declared in the library definition). Libraries that did declare a version used that string directly in the hash, so the aggregate filename was coupled to the version number rather than the file contents.

The fix removes the -1 check and unconditionally calls hash_file('xxh64', $asset['data']) to hash the file contents for every asset item. A file_exists() guard was added: when a file does not exist on disk (for example, "stub" library definitions used in testing), the original version value is kept instead of crashing. The switch from hash('xxh64', file_get_contents(...)) to hash_file(...) was a minor optimization suggested during review that avoids reading the entire file into memory.

The related issue #3505776 (45-day aggregate retention) is the direct context: because aggregates are now kept for weeks rather than regenerated on every cache clear, a version-string-driven hash that changed with every core release was causing unnecessary aggregate churn, while a stale version string could silently serve outdated content.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 8, 2026
  • Committed: May 29, 2026 (21 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • heddn (MTech, LLC)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

During review, smustgrave reported file_get_contents() failures for missing files (both a custom theme CSS file and a core test-theme file), and catch responded by adding the file_exists() guard to keep hash generation from breaking when library definitions reference non-existent files. alexpott suggested swapping hash('xxh64', file_get_contents(...)) for the more efficient hash_file(...), which heddn applied to the MR.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3280235: Calling code can not collect bubbled attachments from TextProcessed
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +152/−1 · php +152/−1
  • Diff: f05f7ab
  • Discussion: #3280235 · 5 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

When Drupal processes a text field through a filter format, individual filter plugins can attach JavaScript libraries and CSS via FilterProcessResult. If you accessed the processed text via $entity->text_field->processed and manually built a render array from it, those attachments were silently lost. Only cache metadata (tags, contexts, max-age) was accessible through CacheableDependencyInterface. The #attachments were trapped inside the internal FilterProcessResult object with no public way to retrieve them. This fix makes TextProcessed also implement AttachmentsInterface, exposing getAttachments(), addAttachments(), and setAttachments() so callers can now collect and apply filter-added libraries to their render arrays.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: TextProcessed now implements AttachmentsInterface, exposing getAttachments(), addAttachments(), and setAttachments() on the computed processed text typed data object.

Technical details

TextProcessed (in core/modules/text/src/TextProcessed.php) now declares implements CacheableDependencyInterface, AttachmentsInterface and pulls in AttachmentsTrait for the default addAttachments() and setAttachments() implementations. The key addition is a custom getAttachments() override that first calls $this->getValue() to trigger the filter pipeline (populating the internal $this->processed FilterProcessResult object), then calls $this->addAttachments($this->processed->getAttachments()) to merge the filter's bubbled attachments into the TextProcessed instance's own attachment storage before returning it. Without this step, FilterProcessResult's attachments were only reachable by the render pipeline when the text was rendered through a #type => 'processed_text' render element; direct access via the computed property provided no route to them. Two tests were added: a unit test (TextProcessedTest) verifying addAttachments() merges correctly, and a kernel test (TextProcessedAttachmentsTest) using the filter_test_assets filter to confirm that a real filter's library (filter/caption) appears in getAttachments() results after processing.

Upgrade

Modules or themes that manually render formatted text fields can now collect attachments alongside cache metadata:

$processed = $entity->text_field->processed;

$cache = new BubbleableMetadata();
$cache->addCacheableDependency($processed);
// New: collect filter-plugin JS/CSS attachments.
$cache->addAttachments($processed->getAttachments());
$cache->applyTo($a_render_array);

The workaround of using a #type => 'processed_text' render element (which lets the render pipeline bubble attachments automatically) remains valid and continues to work unchanged.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 12, 2022
  • Committed: May 29, 2026 (4 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • dcam
  • godotislate
  • a.dmitriiev (1xINTERNET)

Collaboration

The issue sat open for three years before smustgrave opened a merge request implementing AttachmentsInterface on TextProcessed. After dcam reviewed and approved that patch, godotislate identified a critical gap: the initial implementation added the interface and trait but did not override getAttachments() to pull attachments from the underlying FilterProcessResult, meaning the fix did not actually solve the reported problem. godotislate posted the specific override needed in a comment, a.dmitriiev then contributed a kernel test using a real filter to cover the corrected behaviour, and godotislate ultimately committed the combined result.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585716: EntityQuery uses hard-coded langcode in revision-data joins, breaking translatable revisionable entities with custom langcode keys
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +26/−1 · php +26/−1
  • Diff: 2da39e6
  • Discussion: #3585716 · 3 contributors · 17 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

EntityQuery was generating broken SQL for custom entity types that are both translatable and revisionable when the entity type defines a langcode key other than the default langcode (for example, language). The JOIN condition on the revision data table always referenced the literal column name langcode, causing an "Unknown column" SQL error on translation and revision pages. This fix corrects the column name used in those JOIN conditions so it matches the entity type's actual configured langcode key.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The bug lived in the addJoin() method of the SQL entity query backend. When building a JOIN condition that filters by language, the code checked whether the table being joined was the entity's data table (getDataTable()) and, if so, fetched the configured langcode key via $entity_type->getKey('langcode'). For all other tables, including the revision data table (getRevisionDataTable()), it fell back to the hard-coded string 'langcode'. The revision data table, like the data table, stores per-language row data and must also use the entity type's actual langcode key.

The fix replaces the single ternary with a match() expression that maps both getDataTable() and getRevisionDataTable() to $entity_type->getKey('langcode'), leaving the 'langcode' literal as the default case for dedicated field tables (which do hard-code that column name).

The accompanying test modifies the active definition of entity_test_mulrev to use 'language' as its langcode key at runtime, issues an allRevisions() query, and asserts the generated SQL contains "entity_test_mulrev_property_revision"."language" in the JOIN condition rather than "entity_test_mulrev_property_revision"."langcode". No public API surface changed; only internal SQL generation logic in the entity query system was corrected.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 20, 2026
  • Committed: May 29, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • lisotton
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • godotislate
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3569674: Fix remaining PHPStan missingType.generics errors (level 1)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 15 files, +33/−155 · php +33/−155
  • Diff: 938b3f3
  • Discussion: #3569674 · 4 contributors · 12 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Drupal's PHPStan baseline file previously suppressed 17 missingType.generics errors across several core interfaces and classes. This change resolves those suppressions by adding proper generic type parameters directly to PHPDoc return-type annotations. No runtime behavior changes. Developers running PHPStan at level 1 or above on contrib or custom modules that implement or extend the affected public interfaces may now encounter new missingType.generics warnings in their own code, because PHPStan will expect implementing classes to declare matching generic types.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: Generic type parameters added to PHPDoc on FormatterInterface (@template T), FieldableEntityInterface::get/getFields/getTranslatableFields, TypedDataInterface::getParent/getRoot, TypedConfigManagerInterface::get/createFromNameAndData, and several concrete classes; implementing code may require matching annotations to satisfy PHPStan.

Technical details

In phpstan-baseline.php, 17 missingType.generics suppression entries are removed across files in Component, Core, layout_builder, views, and test directories.

The affected public interfaces and classes receive explicit generic parameters in their PHPDoc. FormatterInterface gains a @template T of FieldItemListInterface declaration; its prepareView(), view(), and viewElements() methods use @phpstan-param T $items. FieldableEntityInterface::get() now returns FieldItemListInterface<FieldItemInterface>, and getFields()/getTranslatableFields() return array<string, FieldItemListInterface<FieldItemInterface>>. TypedConfigManagerInterface::get() and createFromNameAndData() return TraversableTypedDataInterface<int|string, TypedDataInterface>. TypedDataInterface::getParent() and getRoot() gain the same parameterization, as do the corresponding methods in TypedData. FormatterPluginManager::getInstance(), FieldBlock::getFormatter() (layout_builder), and views\EntityField::getFormatterInstance() all return FormatterInterface<FieldItemListInterface<FieldItemInterface>>. ItemList::$list is typed array<int, TypedDataInterface> and getIterator() returns ArrayIterator<int, TypedDataInterface>. DirectoryWithMetadataDiscovery::getDirectoryIterator() returns RecursiveIteratorIterator<SplFileInfo>, and ParamConversionEnhancer::copyRawVariables() returns InputBag<string|int|float|bool|null>.

StaticReflectionClass's PHP reflection stubs (getInterfaces(), getParentClass(), getTraits()) instead receive inline // @phpstan-ignore missingType.generics comments because those methods implement PHP's built-in reflection API and cannot have their signatures changed to carry generic parameters.

Upgrade

If you implement or extend any of the following interfaces in a contrib or custom module and run PHPStan, you may now see missingType.generics errors that were previously suppressed only in core's baseline:

  • FormatterInterface — add a @template or @extends annotation, e.g.:
    /**
 * @extends \Drupal\Core\Field\FormatterInterface<\Drupal\Core\Field\FieldItemListInterface<\Drupal\Core\Field\FieldItemInterface>>
 */
class MyFormatter extends FormatterBase { ... }
  • TypedDataInterface / TypedData — getParent() and getRoot() now return TraversableTypedDataInterface<int|string, TypedDataInterface>.
  • FieldableEntityInterface — getFields() and getTranslatableFields() now return array<string, FieldItemListInterface<FieldItemInterface>>.
  • TypedConfigManagerInterface — get() and createFromNameAndData() now return TraversableTypedDataInterface<int|string, TypedDataInterface>.

If your module already suppresses missingType.generics for these methods in its own PHPStan baseline, you can remove those suppressions after adding the proper generic annotations.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 26, 2026
  • Committed: May 29, 2026 (4 months later)

Key contributors

  • mondrake
  • dcam
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #2922451: [policy no patch] Make it possible to mark plugins as deprecated
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #2922451 · 17 contributors · 62 comments
  • Followers: 27

Summary

This issue established the official Drupal policy for how to deprecate a plugin. Before this, developers marking plugin classes with a PHP @deprecated docblock caused problems: plugin discovery still instantiates annotated classes at various points (e.g. when rendering a configuration form listing all available plugins), so the deprecation notice would fire on every page that listed plugins, not just when the plugin was genuinely used. The agreed policy is to place @trigger_error() in the plugin constructor rather than relying on a class-level deprecation. This ensures the notice fires only when the plugin is actually instantiated, not during discovery or listing operations. The documentation at https://www.drupal.org/core/deprecation#how-plugin was updated with a canonical example.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The core problem was that PHP's process-isolated PHPUnit bridge (landed in #2870194) turns E_USER_DEPRECATED into test failures. Because the action module's configuration form iterates getDefinitions() and calls is_subclass_of() on each class, any plugin class annotated with @deprecated at the class level would immediately trigger a failure during discovery-related operations, even in tests that were not testing the deprecated plugin at all.

The policy that was agreed and documented has three parts:

  1. Add @trigger_error('ClassName is deprecated in drupal:X.Y.Z ...', E_USER_DEPRECATED) as the first statement in the plugin's constructor. If the plugin has no constructor, add one. Placing it in the constructor means it fires only on actual instantiation (createInstance()), not during getDefinitions() or any other reflection-based discovery call.

  2. Add no_ui = true to the plugin annotation definition so the deprecated plugin is excluded from UIs that list plugins for selection. The thread noted that no_ui must be used on a case-by-case basis: some UIs need to keep showing a plugin that already has active configuration even when new instances should not be created.

  3. Add an @deprecated and @see phpdoc annotation to the plugin class docblock so that static analysis tools (IDEs, PHPStan) can also surface the deprecation.

A second option discussed in the thread, putting the trigger in PluginManagerBase::createInstance() or DefaultPluginFactory keyed on a plugin annotation property, was considered but not adopted for this policy. The constructor approach was preferred because it parallels how regular classes and methods are deprecated and works regardless of which factory class is in use. A follow-up issue (#3039240) tracks adding a formal deprecated annotation key to the plugin system for YAML-defined plugins, which cannot use the constructor approach cleanly.

Upgrade

To deprecate a plugin, follow this pattern:

/**
 * Plugin implementation of the 'my_old_plugin' type.
 *
 * @deprecated in drupal:10.1.0 and is removed from drupal:11.0.0. Use
 *   \Drupal\mymodule\Plugin\MyType\NewPlugin instead.
 * @see https://www.drupal.org/node/XXXXXXX
 *
 * @MyPluginAnnotation(
 *   id = "my_old_plugin",
 *   label = @Translation("My old plugin (deprecated)"),
 *   no_ui = true
 * )
 */
class MyOldPlugin extends PluginBase {

  /**
   * Constructs a MyOldPlugin object.
   */
  public function __construct(array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) {
    @trigger_error('\Drupal\mymodule\Plugin\MyType\MyOldPlugin is deprecated in drupal:10.1.0 and is removed from drupal:11.0.0. Use \Drupal\mymodule\Plugin\MyType\NewPlugin instead. See https://www.drupal.org/node/XXXXXXX', E_USER_DEPRECATED);
    parent::__construct($configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition);
  }

}

Key points: place @trigger_error() before parent::__construct(), add no_ui = true to the annotation, and add @deprecated and @see to the class docblock. Whether no_ui effectively hides the plugin in a given UI depends on that UI's implementation; verify on a case-by-case basis.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: November 10, 2017

Key contributors

  • jibran (PreviousNext)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • dawehner (Chapter Three (acquired by Kanopi Studios))
  • eclipsegc (Acquia)
  • heddn (Acro Commerce)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • phenaproxima (Acquia)
  • joachim
  • andypost (Skilld)
  • chr.fritsch (Thunder)
  • tim.plunkett (Acquia)
  • claudiu.cristea (Webikon)

Collaboration

The thread involved a substantive disagreement between EclipseGc and the majority of core maintainers (alexpott, catch, tim.plunkett, berdir). EclipseGc argued that the correct place for the deprecation trigger is DefaultPluginManager::createInstance() (or the factory layer), keyed on an introspectable annotation property, because the plugin system is designed around factories and not manual construction. His concern was that the constructor approach cannot support filtering deprecated plugins from UIs and cannot propagate deprecation knowledge through derivatives. The counter-argument, articulated by catch and alexpott, was that putting it in the constructor closely mirrors how regular PHP classes are deprecated, works across all factory implementations (not just DefaultFactory), and fires correctly at the point of actual use. The constructor approach was chosen, with the note that UI filtering via no_ui should be handled case-by-case and that a formal annotation key for more robust support remains an open follow-up (#3039240).

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3503843: Remove automatic preloading of all "public" routes, cache routes in fast chained bin
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 11 files, +97/−385 · php +90/−381 · yml +7/−4
  • Diff: 01c9d71
  • Discussion: #3503843 · 7 contributors · 69 comments
  • Followers: 18

Summary

Drupal used to bulk-load all "public" (non-admin GET/HTML) routes into memory on every HTML request through a RoutePreloader event subscriber. On larger sites this meant loading 100 to 200 or more serialized route objects, consuming roughly 1.5 MB of memory per request. That subscriber and its associated state key (routing.non_admin_routes) have been removed. Routes are now cached individually in a new dedicated cache.routes bin backed by the chainedfast backend (APCu + persistent storage), and loaded on demand using getMultiple() rather than pre-fetched in bulk. The net effect is a significant reduction in memory usage per request and faster cache reads when routes are warm in APCu, with a small increase in the number of cache lookups on cold caches that is outweighed by the cost of the eliminated bulk load.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: Post-update system_post_update_remove_non_admin_routes_state() removes the routing.non_admin_routes state key
  • Deprecation: RouteProvider::$serializedRoutes and RouteProvider::ROUTE_LOAD_CID_PREFIX deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:12.0.0; no replacement
  • New API: New cache.routes cache bin (chained fast backend) introduced for per-route caching

Technical details

The old approach stored all non-admin routes under a single cache item keyed by a sha512 hash of the route name list (using ROUTE_LOAD_CID_PREFIX). These were stored as serialized strings in cache.data and held in RouteProvider::$serializedRoutes until needed, then deserialized on access. The RoutePreloader subscriber populated a routing.non_admin_routes state key during route rebuilds and triggered the bulk cache load on KernelEvents::REQUEST for every HTML request.

The new approach introduces a cache.routes service defined in core.services.yml with default_backend: cache.backend.chainedfast. RouteProvider is now injected with this bin instead of cache.data. preLoadRoutes() calls cache->getMultiple() with the route names as cache IDs, routes not found in cache are fetched from the router database table and immediately deserialized, then written back via cache->setMultiple() with the routes cache tag. The intermediate serialized-string holding area ($serializedRoutes) is eliminated; routes enter $this->routes as objects directly.

RouteProvider::$serializedRoutes and RouteProvider::ROUTE_LOAD_CID_PREFIX are deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:12.0.0 (see change record https://www.drupal.org/node/3589089). The RoutePreloader class and router.route_preloader service are deleted outright with no BC layer, as event subscribers are explicitly excluded from Drupal's BC policy. A post-update function system_post_update_remove_non_admin_routes_state() cleans up the now-unused routing.non_admin_routes state entry. A parallel effort to add an explicit TTL to the APCu fast bin to bound memory growth is tracked in #3586418.

Upgrade

If your custom code references RouteProvider::$serializedRoutes or RouteProvider::ROUTE_LOAD_CID_PREFIX, remove those usages; there is no replacement. If you registered a service that depends on router.route_preloader, remove that dependency. The RoutePreloader class no longer exists.

// Deprecated — remove any reference to:
RouteProvider::ROUTE_LOAD_CID_PREFIX
$routeProvider->serializedRoutes  // protected, but if subclassed

// Removed service — remove from any service definitions:
// router.route_preloader

The new cache.routes bin can be overridden like any other cache bin in settings.php:

$settings['cache']['bins']['routes'] = 'cache.backend.apcu';

Sites running on shared or memory-constrained APCu should review available APCu memory, as individual route objects will now populate it instead of a handful of bulk cache items.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 2, 2025
  • Committed: May 28, 2026 (1 year and 3 commits later)

Collaboration

berdir opened the issue after profiling a production site and finding that route preloading consumed around 1.5 MB per request and triggered dozens of redundant cache lookups. They ran multiple rounds of profiling across anonymous and authenticated page views, explored several caching strategies (fast-only, per-format, combined with getMultiple()), and shared detailed query counts and memory measurements from Blackfire to guide the design.

catch independently opened a draft MR (MR !11105) to test the approach without caching, sharing observations about which specific routes drove most lookups (the is_front logic, <current>, bigpipe). Those experiments fed back into berdir's implementation without replacing it. alexpott's review raised the question of deprecating preLoadRoutes() and PreloadableRouteProviderInterface outright; berdir explored it, concluded it added complexity that belonged in a follow-up, and opened #3589340 for that discussion. longwave provided the final RTBC after reviewing the MR.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3592406: Mark Default Admin beta and revert the revert
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 100 files, +12685/−0 · css +12496 · yml +189
  • Diff: d8905dd
  • Discussion: #3592406 · 3 contributors · 8 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The default_admin admin theme has been restored to Drupal core on 11.4.x and marked as beta. It was briefly removed in a prior commit but is now re-added so that early adopters can test it before Drupal 12, where Claro is planned to be removed. Site owners can enable the theme now to assess compatibility and provide feedback while it is still in beta.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New core beta admin theme default_admin available in core/themes/default_admin, intended as the eventual replacement for Claro in Drupal 12.

Technical details

The commit reverses a prior removal of core/themes/default_admin by re-applying the original addition patch (using the reverse of commit 2e658715f940d1fe6260bc7a4e0f99ff7cf15d4a), excluding unrelated files (such as the mailer module) that were part of the same original patch but were intentionally left removed.

The restored theme ships with a full set of default block placement config files (breadcrumbs, content, help, local actions, messages, page title, primary/secondary local tasks), a default_admin.settings config schema covering accent color, focus color, dark mode, high contrast mode, layout density, and per-user theme override settings. The CSS layer uses CSS custom properties under the [data-gin-accent] attribute selector and includes polyfill variables that map to Gin-compatible token names (e.g. --gin-color-primary, --gin-bg-app), supporting both color-mix() and @supports-gated relative color syntax fallbacks. The font stack uses a variable Ginter font face (the Inter variable font). The default_admin.info.yml marks the theme's lifecycle as beta.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 27, 2026
  • Committed: May 28, 2026 (1 day later)

Key contributors

  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

gábor hojtsy manually verified the MR by checking out the branch, applying the reverse of the original removal patch, and confirming that only core/themes/default_admin files were restored while the mailer module files (which were in the same original patch) correctly remained absent. This manual verification provided the confidence needed to merge quickly.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3592341: Fix dot-segment encoding for chained "../" and "./" in generated URLs
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +33/−6 · php +33/−6
  • Diff: eb2964e
  • Discussion: #3592341 · 5 contributors · 31 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

Drupal's routing system maintains its own copy of Symfony's URL generation logic in UrlGenerator::generateFromRoute(). That copy contained the same vulnerability described in Symfony CVE-2026-48784: when a route parameter contains chained dot-segments such as ../../.. or ././., the URL generator failed to percent-encode all of them, leaving raw ../ or ./ sequences in the generated URL. A browser or HTTP client following such a URL would resolve the dot-segments as path traversal. This fix ensures every . and .. segment is encoded to %2E and %2E%2E respectively, regardless of how many are chained. The companion issue #3592421 updated the upstream Symfony packages; this issue fixes Drupal's own forked copy.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Security: Incomplete percent-encoding of chained ../ and ./ dot-segments in UrlGenerator::generateFromRoute() could allow path traversal via crafted route parameters (Symfony CVE-2026-48784).

Technical details

The old code in UrlGenerator::generateFromRoute() used strtr($path, ['/../' => '/%2E%2E/', '/./' => '/%2E/']) plus two str_ends_with checks for trailing /. and /... Because strtr() performs a single non-overlapping scan, a sequence like /../../.. would only encode some of the segments: the replacement of the first /../ consumed the surrounding slashes, leaving the adjacent ../ without leading or trailing slashes to match on the next pass. The test run by smustgrave confirmed the symptom: expected /test-path/%2E%2E/%2E%2E/%2E%2E/bar but received /test-path/%2E%2E/../%2E%2E/bar.

The fix, cherry-picked from Symfony commit 4b63c3a3, replaces the strtr approach with an explicit segment-by-segment pass: explode('/', $path), encode any segment that is exactly . or .., then implode('/', $segments). This handles any number of consecutive dot-segments and also covers segments at the tail of the path without special-casing. Tests covering all three chained patterns (../../.., ././., ../././..) and tail routes were ported directly from the Symfony commit.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 27, 2026
  • Committed: May 28, 2026

Key contributors

  • znerol
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • dww (3281d Consulting)
  • godotislate
  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

After the initial MR, smustgrave asked whether test coverage should be added. znerol ported the tests from the upstream Symfony commit and pushed them. smustgrave then ran them locally and caught that the tests actually failed against the existing code, posting the specific mismatch between expected and actual output. That confirmed the real scope of the bug and validated the need for the fix. dww reviewed the Symfony commit and the MR together and bumped the priority to Major given the CVE origin.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3592421: Update Symfony routing and http-foundation versions
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 10.5.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +20/−20 · lock +15/−15 · json +5/−5
  • Diff: 9f017a5
  • Discussion: #3592421 · 5 contributors · 38 comments
  • Followers: 11

Summary

Two Symfony security vulnerabilities are addressed by bumping symfony/http-foundation and symfony/routing to version ^8.0.13 across all supported Drupal branches (10.5.x through main). Sites that run composer audit before this update will see CVE-2026-48736 and CVE-2026-48784 reported; after updating they will not. Both CVEs were disclosed on 2026-05-26.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Security: CVE-2026-48736 (SSRF bypass via IPv6 omission in symfony/http-foundation) and CVE-2026-48784 (URL path traversal via dot-segment encoding in symfony/routing) patched by requiring both packages at ^8.0.13.

Technical details

CVE-2026-48736 is in symfony/http-foundation: IpUtils::PRIVATE_SUBNETS omitted IPv6 transition address forms (6to4, NAT64, Teredo, IPv4-compatible), allowing an SSRF bypass via NoPrivateNetworkHttpClient. The fix raises the minimum required version from ^8.0 (previously pinned to v8.0.8 in the lockfile) to ^8.0.13.

CVE-2026-48784 is in symfony/routing: UrlGenerator dot-segment encoding skipped every other chained ../ or ./ sequence, potentially collapsing a generated URL off-route under RFC 3986 normalization. The fix raises the minimum from ^8.0.12 to ^8.0.13. Note that Drupal ships its own UrlGenerator subclass, so fully covering CVE-2026-48784 for Drupal also requires the separate fix tracked in #3592341.

The diff touches core/composer.json, composer/Metapackage/CoreRecommended/composer.json, composer/Metapackage/PinnedDevDependencies/composer.json, and composer.lock. No PHP source files outside of Composer metadata were changed.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 27, 2026
  • Committed: May 28, 2026

Key contributors

  • godotislate
  • dww (3281d Consulting)
  • znerol
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • catch (Third and Grove)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3592065: Drupal core-recommended pins vulnerable symfony/polyfill-intl-idn version affected by CVE-2026-46644
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 10.5.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +7/−7 · lock +6/−6 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: 56aee63
  • Discussion: #3592065 · 16 contributors · 34 comments
  • Followers: 34

Summary

The drupal/core-recommended package pinned symfony/polyfill-intl-idn at ~v1.37.0, which is affected by CVE-2026-46644 (insecure equivalence in IDN handling for ASCII-only xn-- labels). Because the pin prevented Composer from resolving a newer version, sites using drupal/core-recommended could not update the vulnerable package on their own. This change raises the pin to ~v1.38.1, the first non-vulnerable release, so a normal composer update will pull in the patched version. The fix was backported to 10.5.x, 10.6.x, 11.2.x, 11.3.x, 11.4.x, 11.x, and main.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Security: CVE-2026-46644: insecure IDN equivalence in symfony/polyfill-intl-idn versions prior to 1.38.1; drupal/core-recommended pin updated to require the patched release.

Technical details

The entire change is in composer.json (the version constraint for symfony/polyfill-intl-idn updated from ~v1.37.0 to ~v1.38.1) and the corresponding composer.lock entry (version bumped from v1.37.0 to v1.38.1, git reference updated to dc21118016c039a66235cf93d96b435ffb282412). No Drupal PHP code was modified. The vulnerability is in the polyfill's IDN-to-ASCII conversion, which incorrectly treats some xn-- ACE labels as equivalent when they are not. Sites running PHP with the native intl extension enabled are not affected at runtime because the polyfill checks for the extension and defers to it; the vulnerability only triggers when the polyfill's own PHP implementation is used.

Upgrade

After updating to a Drupal core release that includes this fix, run:

composer update drupal/core-recommended symfony/polyfill-intl-idn -W

Until such a release is available, a temporary workaround is to add an alias in your root composer.json:

"require-dev": {
    "symfony/polyfill-intl-idn": "1.38.1 as 1.37.0"
}

Or replace drupal/core-recommended with drupal/core in your root require and add "symfony/polyfill-intl-idn": "^1.38.1" explicitly. Alternatively, enabling the PHP intl extension on your server bypasses the vulnerable polyfill code at runtime without any Composer changes.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 26, 2026
  • Committed: May 26, 2026

Key contributors

  • ahmetburkan
  • godotislate
  • znerol

Collaboration

julien tekrane documented in detail a working workaround (switching from drupal/core-recommended to drupal/core and adding the explicit constraint) while the fix was in progress. mwebaze pointed out that enabling the native intl PHP extension neutralises the vulnerability at runtime regardless of which polyfill version is installed, which several site owners found useful. godotislate prepared and tested the MR against main and confirmed cherry-picks to 11.x, 11.4.x, 11.3.x, and 10.6.x all resolved cleanly before the fix was committed.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
